message:
fix(ft-conformance): give Adam custom-function test enough iterations to converge (frankentorch-mbl)

custom_function_layer_trains_end_to_end_with_adam was running an Adam
fit with lr=0.05, init weight=0.25 / bias=0.0, target affine w=3 /
b=1, for 80 optimizer steps and asserting |pred - target| < 0.2 on
each element. On a clean main checkout the worst element landed at
|delta|≈0.22, just outside the 0.2 threshold — deterministic failure
across the rch fleet (frankentorch-mbl reality-check note).

The numerics weren't the problem; 80 steps was simply right at the
convergence boundary for that init/lr combo. Bump the iteration
budget to 200, which lets Adam reach a comfortable interior of the
acceptance region without changing thresholds, init, learning rate,
or any of the gradient/loss machinery being exercised. Inline comment
records the frankentorch-mbl context so a future reader knows why 200
and not "round number".

Of the three remediation paths captured in frankentorch-mbl (seed +
re-tune threshold, raise iteration budget, loosen per-element
threshold with rationale), this is option #2 — the least surface
change and the one that preserves the test's original convergence
acceptance bar.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
